Simple and fascinating. The Greeks and Romans were doing it thousands of years ago. Each bottle is the result of climate, soil, grape variety, and the winemaker's craft.
¿Qué es el vino?.tip: To the guest: "Our wines are like a journey through Spain — each glass tells the story of a place and a tradition."

Tinto, Blanco y Rosado.title: Red, White and Rosé
Tinto, Blanco y Rosado.short: The wine's color depends on contact with the grape skin.
Tinto, Blanco y Rosado.detail: Why is one wine red and another white? The key is in the grape skin. Reds ferment WITH the skin (which contains pigments), so they absorb color, tannins and body. Whites are made WITHOUT the skin, so they are lighter and fresher. Rosé is a middle ground: the skin touches the juice for just a few hours, giving it that beautiful pink hue.
Tinto, Blanco y Rosado.tip: To the guest: "Prefer something lighter and fresher? A white. More body and intensity? A red. Something in between? Our Muga rosé is perfect."

Las 4 cosas que notarás al probar.title: The 4 things you'll notice
Las 4 cosas que notarás al probar.short: Sweetness, acidity, tannins and body: the 4 basic wine sensations.
Las 4 cosas que notarás al probar.detail: You don't need to be an expert to enjoy wine. Just pay attention to 4 things: 1) SWEETNESS: is it dry or sweet? 2) ACIDITY: does it make you salivate? Like biting a lemon. Whites usually have more. 3) TANNINS: do you feel dryness in your mouth? Only in reds. Like biting a grape skin. 4) BODY: does it feel light like water or dense like milk? That simple.
Las 4 cosas que notarás al probar.tip: To the guest: "I invite you to notice how this wine feels in your mouth — if you feel freshness, that's the grape's natural acidity."

Cómo catar en 3 pasos.title: How to taste in 3 steps
Cómo catar en 3 pasos.short: Sight, nose and palate: the ritual to enjoy any wine.
Cómo catar en 3 pasos.detail: Tasting wine is easier than it seems: STEP 1 — LOOK: tilt the glass over something white. Is it pale or intense? Young reds are purple, old ones are garnet. STEP 2 — SMELL: put your nose in the glass. First without swirling (gentle aromas), then swirl and smell again (deeper aromas). STEP 3 — TASTE: take a small sip and move it around your mouth. What fruits do you notice? Fresh or warm?
Cómo catar en 3 pasos.tip: To the guest: "If you like, I'll share a secret: gently swirl the glass before smelling — that way the wine releases all its hidden aromas."

¿Por qué importa la uva?.title: Why the grape matters
¿Por qué importa la uva?.short: Each grape variety gives completely different flavors.
¿Por qué importa la uva?.detail: Just as there are green apples (tart) and red apples (sweet), there are very different wine grapes. TEMPRANILLO (Spain's star) produces elegant reds with notes of red fruit and leather. ALBARIÑO gives crisp whites that smell of peach and flowers. GARNACHA produces powerful, spicy reds. At Txoko we have wines from over 15 different varieties — it's like having a flavor map.
¿Por qué importa la uva?.tip: To the guest: "This wine is made with Tempranillo, Spain's most famous grape — it will give you notes of cherry, vanilla and a spicy touch."

¿Qué vino pido con mi plato?.title: What wine with my dish?
¿Qué vino pido con mi plato?.short: The easy rule: white with fish, red with meat, and whatever you want!
¿Qué vino pido con mi plato?.detail: Don't overthink it: the basic rule works 80% of the time. FISH and SEAFOOD (tataki, sea bass, grouper) → fresh whites or sparkling. MEATS (sirloin, lamb, burger) → reds. DESSERTS (brownie, mousse) → sweet wines. But the most important rule is: THERE ARE NO RULES. If you like red with fish, go for it! The best pairing is the one you enjoy.
¿Qué vino pido con mi plato?.tip: To the guest: "I'd recommend a fresh white with your fish, but if you prefer red, we have elegant options that also work very well."

¿Copa o botella?.title: Glass or bottle?
¿Copa o botella?.short: By the glass is perfect to try and discover without commitment.
¿Copa o botella?.detail: At Txoko we have more than 10 wines available by the glass — the perfect way to try wines without committing to a whole bottle. A glass is ideal if: you're dining alone or as a couple, you want to try something new, each person wants a different wine, or you simply want a glass with your dish. Prices range from €10 to €25 per glass, so you can explore without spending much.
¿Copa o botella?.tip: To the guest: "If you'd like to try without commitment, we have a selection of wines by the glass starting at €10. Perfect for discovering new favorites."

Crianza, Reserva y Gran Reserva.title: Crianza, Reserva and Gran Reserva
Crianza, Reserva y Gran Reserva.short: The 3 aging categories of Spanish wine.
Crianza, Reserva y Gran Reserva.detail: Spain classifies its wines by aging time. CRIANZA: minimum 2 years total, 6 months in barrel (12 in Rioja). Result: notes of vanilla and mild spice. RESERVA: minimum 3 years, 12 months in barrel. More complexity and depth. GRAN RESERVA: minimum 5 years, 18 months in barrel. Only in the best vintages. Gems. Easy summary: more time = more elegant and complex.
Crianza, Reserva y Gran Reserva.tip: To the guest: "This Crianza has an elegant touch of oak. If you want more complexity, our Reservas have more depth, like Marqués de Murrieta."

Denominación de Origen (DO).title: Denominación de Origen (DO)
Denominación de Origen (DO).short: Seal guaranteeing the wine's geographic origin and quality.
Denominación de Origen (DO).detail: The DO certifies that a wine comes from a specific region and meets controlled quality standards. Each DO has its own rules about permitted grape varieties, production methods and aging time.
Denominación de Origen (DO).tip: To the guest: "This seal guarantees the wine comes from where it says and has been made following that region's tradition."

DOCa / DOQ (Calificada).title: DOCa / DOQ (Qualified)
DOCa / DOQ (Calificada).short: The highest denomination level in Spain. Only Rioja and Priorat.
DOCa / DOQ (Calificada).detail: Only two regions in Spain hold this top distinction: Rioja (DOCa since 1991) and Priorat (DOQ since 2000). It implies stricter controls throughout the production chain, from vineyard to bottling.
DOCa / DOQ (Calificada).tip: To the guest: "It's the elite of Spanish wines. Only two regions in all of Spain have this recognition."

Vinos Canarios.title: Canary Island Wines
Vinos Canarios.short: Unique volcanic vineyards worldwide, with pre-phylloxera native varieties.
Vinos Canarios.detail: The Canary Islands are a global wine treasure. Their vines were never affected by the phylloxera plague that devastated Europe in the 19th century, so they preserve centuries-old vines on their own roots. The volcanic soil and Atlantic winds create unique mineral and saline wines. Varieties like Listán Negro, Listán Blanco, Malvasía, Marmajuelo and Albillo Criollo are found nowhere else.
Vinos Canarios.tip: To the guest: "Our Canary wines are unique in the world — born from centuries-old volcanic vineyards that survived the plague that destroyed Europe's vineyards. They are literally living history in every glass."

Taninos.title: Tannins
Taninos.short: Compounds that give structure and a drying sensation on the palate.
Taninos.detail: Tannins come from grape skin, seeds and oak barrels. They are responsible for that astringent or "drying" sensation in the mouth. With aging, tannins soften and the wine gains elegance.
Taninos.tip: To the guest: "If you feel a slight dryness on the palate, those are the tannins — that's what gives the wine body and structure."

Maridaje.title: Food Pairing
Maridaje.short: The art of combining wine and food to elevate both.
Maridaje.detail: Pairing aims for wine and dish to complement each other. Basic rules: fresh whites with fish and seafood, reds with meats and stews, sweet wines with desserts. But the most important rule is: the perfect pairing is the one you enjoy.
Maridaje.tip: To the guest: "I recommend this wine because its freshness/body/acidity perfectly complements the flavors of your dish."

Decantación.title: Decanting
Decantación.short: Aerating the wine so it develops all its aromas.
Decantación.detail: To decant a wine means to transfer it from bottle to decanter to oxygenate it. It's especially useful with powerful young reds (to soften them) and with old wines (to separate sediments). Decanting time varies: 30 minutes for young wines, just a few minutes for very old ones.
Decantación.tip: To the guest: "Allow me to decant this wine — oxygenating it will reveal all its hidden aromas."

Temperatura de servicio.title: Serving temperature
Temperatura de servicio.short: Every wine has its ideal temperature to be enjoyed at its best.
Temperatura de servicio.detail: Light whites: 7-9°C. Full-bodied whites and rosés: 10-12°C. Young reds: 14-16°C. Oak-aged reds: 16-18°C. Sparkling: 6-8°C. Sweet: 6-8°C. A wine too cold loses aromas, too warm loses freshness.
Temperatura de servicio.tip: To the guest: "I'm serving this [red/white] at its ideal temperature so you can appreciate every nuance."

Variedades autóctonas españolas.title: Spanish native varieties
Variedades autóctonas españolas.short: Spain has over 600 grape varieties, many unique in the world.
Variedades autóctonas españolas.detail: Star reds: Tempranillo (Rioja, Ribera del Duero), Garnacha (Aragón, Priorat), Monastrell (Jumilla), Mencía (Bierzo), Listán Negro (Canary Islands). Star whites: Albariño (Rías Baixas), Verdejo (Rueda), Godello (Valdeorras), Viura (Rioja), Malvasía (Canarias, Lanzarote). Each expresses its terroir uniquely.
Variedades autóctonas españolas.tip: To the guest: "Spain has the greatest grape diversity in the world. This wine is made with [variety], which only reaches this expression in [region]."

Copa adecuada.title: The right glass
Copa adecuada.short: The glass shape directly influences how we perceive the wine.
Copa adecuada.detail: Large, wide glass for full-bodied reds (allows oxygenation). Narrower glass for whites (preserves freshness). Flute or tulip glass for sparkling (preserves bubbles). Always hold the glass by the stem so you don't warm the wine.
Copa adecuada.tip: To the guest: "We use this specific glass so you can fully appreciate the aromas and the wine's ideal temperature."

Islas Canarias.summary: Pre-phylloxera volcanic vineyards, grape varieties unique in the world
Islas Canarias.script: Our Canary wines are a genuine wine treasure. The vines grow in volcanic soil, sometimes in small pits dug into the lava to protect them from the wind — a landscape UNESCO has recognized as World Heritage. Most special of all: these vines were never affected by phylloxera, so they are centuries-old vines on their original roots. The result: mineral, saline wines that taste of Atlantic and volcano.
Islas Canarias.keyGrapes: Listán Blanco, Listán Negro, Malvasía, Marmajuelo, Albillo Criollo

La Rioja.summary: Cradle of Spanish wine, a century-old tradition of excellence
La Rioja.script: La Rioja is Spain's most prestigious wine region, with DOCa (Qualified) status — the highest distinction. Centuries-old wineries like Marqués de Murrieta (founded in 1852) combine tradition with innovation. The continental climate, moderated by the Sierra de Cantabria, creates perfect conditions for Tempranillo, which here reaches its maximum expression with elegance and longevity.

Ribera del Duero.summary: Powerful, elegant reds at high altitude
Ribera del Duero.script: Ribera del Duero produces some of Spain's most powerful reds. Its vineyards are among the highest in Europe (700-1000m), creating an extreme day-night temperature swing. Tempranillo (locally called Tinto Fino or Tinta del País) develops unique concentration and intensity. Vega Sicilia wines, whose Tempos group distributes Alion, are global icons.

Galicia.script: Galicia is the land of Albariño and Godello, two of Spain's most exciting white grapes. The humid, cool Atlantic climate produces wines of vibrant acidity and a minerality that recalls the sea. Rías Baixas (Albariño) makes wines that pair perfectly with seafood, while Valdeorras (Godello) surprises with whites of great structure and elegance.

Priorat.summary: Mineral reds from slate terrace vineyards
Priorat.script: Priorat is one of only two DOCa regions in Spain. Its vineyards grow on steep licorella (slate) terraces, sometimes with 60% slopes. Old Garnacha and Cariñena vines produce minimal yields but extraordinary concentration. These are wines of great intensity, yet also of a unique mineral elegance.

Francia.summary: World references from Burgundy, Bordeaux and the Rhône
Francia.script: Our French selection covers three iconic regions. From Burgundy we have Pinot Noir and Chardonnay of supreme elegance. From the Rhône, Grenache and Syrah wines with body and spice. And from Bordeaux, classic blends of Cabernet and Merlot. We also offer Champagne, the world reference for sparkling wine.
Francia.keyGrapes: Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Syrah, Grenache, Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿De qué está hecho el vino?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What is wine made of?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: De zumo de uva fermentado. La levadura transforma el azúcar de la uva en alcohol.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Fermented grape juice. Yeast transforms the grape's sugar into alcohol.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Es más simple de lo que parece

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Por qué el vino tinto es rojo?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: Why is red wine red?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Porque fermenta CON la piel de la uva, que tiene pigmentos rojos/morados. El blanco se hace SIN piel.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Because it ferments WITH the grape skin, which has red/purple pigments. White wine is made WITHOUT skin.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: La clave está en la piel
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: The key is in the skin

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Cuáles son los 3 pasos para catar un vino?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What are the 3 steps of wine tasting?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: 1) VISTA: mira el color. 2) NARIZ: huele los aromas. 3) BOCA: prueba y siente las sensaciones.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: 1) SIGHT: look at the color. 2) NOSE: smell the aromas. 3) PALATE: taste and feel the sensations.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Usa tus sentidos en orden

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué tipo de vino va mejor con pescado?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What type of wine pairs best with fish?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Generalmente vinos blancos frescos o espumosos. Por ejemplo, con el Tataki de atún un Cepado o Fenomenal SB.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Generally fresh white wines or sparkling. For example, with tuna tataki a Cepado or Fenomenal SB.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Piensa en frescura y ligereza

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué tipo de vino va mejor con carne roja?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What type of wine pairs best with red meat?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Generalmente vinos tintos con cuerpo. Con la Carrillera de Wagyu, por ejemplo, un Marqués de Murrieta Reserva.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Generally full-bodied red wines. With Wagyu cheek, for example, a Marqués de Murrieta Reserva.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Cuerpo con cuerpo

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Cuántos vinos por copa ofrece Txoko?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: How many wines by the glass does Txoko offer?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Más de 10 vinos por copa, desde 10€ (Arienzo, Valtravieso Nogara) hasta 25€ (Andre Clouet Champagne).
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Over 10 wines by the glass, from 10€ (Arienzo, Valtravieso Nogara) to 25€ (Andre Clouet Champagne).
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Es una buena opción para probar sin compromiso
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: A great option to try without commitment

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué es un vino "seco"?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What is a "dry" wine?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Un vino sin dulzor. La mayoría de tintos y blancos de nuestra carta son secos. Lo contrario es un vino dulce como el Tokaji Aszú.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: A wine with no sweetness. Most reds and whites on our list are dry. The opposite is a sweet wine like Tokaji Aszú.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: No tiene que ver con la textura
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: It has nothing to do with texture
distractors: Un vino elaborado con uvas pasificadas al sol antes de prensarlas, lo que concentra sus azúcares y da como resultado un vino denso y muy aromático.
Un vino que ha perdido gas y frescura por llevar la botella demasiado tiempo abierta, y que por eso resulta más áspero y plano en boca al servirlo.
distractors_en: A wine made from grapes dried in the sun before pressing, which concentrates their sugars and gives a dense, intensely aromatic result.
A wine that has lost its gas and freshness because the bottle was left open far too long, and therefore tastes harsher and flatter in the mouth.

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué haces si un huésped dice que no le gusta el vino?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What do you do if a guest says they don't like wine?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Le ofreces alternativas: espumosos o vinos dulces, mocktails sin alcohol (12€), o incluso un espumoso suave como Colet A Priori que puede sorprenderle.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Offer alternatives: sparkling or sweet wines, non-alcoholic mocktails (12€), or even a gentle sparkling like Colet A Priori that might surprise them.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Siempre hay alternativas
distractors: Le insistes en que es una de las joyas de nuestra bodega y le animas a acostumbrarse poco a poco a su sabor, describiéndole con detalle sus notas y aromas antes de plantear siquiera un cambio de botella.
Retiras la copa en silencio y le sirves agua, dando por hecho que ya no querrá probar nada más de nuestra carta de vinos durante el resto de la velada.
distractors_en: You insist that it is one of the true jewels of our cellar and encourage the guest to get used to its flavour little by little, describing its aromas in detail before you even consider changing the bottle.
You quietly take the glass away and pour water instead, simply assuming the guest will no longer want to try anything else from our wine list for the rest of the evening.

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué significa que un vino se sirve "por copa"?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What does it mean when a wine is served "by the glass"?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Que puedes pedir solo una copa en lugar de la botella entera. Ideal para probar o si cada comensal quiere algo diferente.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: You can order just one glass instead of the whole bottle. Ideal for tasting or if each guest wants something different.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: No hace falta comprar toda la botella
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: No need to buy the whole bottle
distractors: Significa que la botella se abrió antes para otra mesa y ahora se ofrece más barata antes de que se estropee del todo, así que conviene beberla cuanto antes.
Significa que el sumiller sirve una medida de cata idéntica para toda la mesa, de modo que todos los comensales comparten exactamente el mismo vino a la vez.
distractors_en: It means the bottle was opened earlier for another table and is now offered at a lower price before it spoils completely, so it should really be drunk quickly.
It means the sommelier pours one identical tasting measure for the whole table, so that every guest shares exactly the same wine at the very same time.

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Cuál es la uva tinta más famosa de España?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What is Spain's most famous red grape?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: El Tempranillo. Es la estrella de Rioja y Ribera del Duero. Da vinos elegantes con notas de fruta roja, vainilla y cuero.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Tempranillo. It's the star of Rioja and Ribera del Duero. It produces elegant wines with notes of red fruit, vanilla and leather.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Piensa en La Rioja
distractors: Garnacha. Muy cultivada en Aragón y el Priorat, da tintos cálidos y de alta graduación, con fruta roja madura y un cuerpo más redondo, suave y generoso en boca.
Monastrell. Variedad sureña de piel gruesa de Jumilla y Yecla, que da vinos oscuros y potentes, de taninos firmes, notas cálidas y un final especiado y largo.
distractors_en: Garnacha. Widely grown across Aragón and Priorat, it produces warm, high-alcohol reds with ripe red-fruit character and a rounder, softer, more generous body on the palate.
Monastrell. A thick-skinned southern grape from Jumilla and Yecla, yielding dark, powerful wines with firm tannin, warm notes and a long, spiced finish.

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Cuántos meses mínimo en barrica necesita un vino para ser Crianza?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: How many months minimum in barrel does a wine need to be Crianza?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: 6 meses (12 meses para tintos en Rioja)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: 6 months (12 months for reds in Rioja)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Piensa en medio año

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Cuántos años de envejecimiento total requiere un Gran Reserva?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: How many years of total ageing does a Gran Reserva require?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Mínimo 5 años (60 meses), con al menos 18 meses en barrica
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Minimum 5 years (60 months), with at least 18 months in barrel
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Es la categoría más alta

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Cuáles son las únicas dos regiones DOCa/DOQ de España?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What are the only two DOCa/DOQ regions in Spain?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Rioja (DOCa desde 1991) y Priorat (DOQ desde 2000)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Rioja (DOCa since 1991) and Priorat (DOQ since 2000)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Una está en La Rioja, otra en Cataluña
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: One is in La Rioja, the other in Catalonia

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué hace únicos a los viñedos canarios respecto al resto de Europa?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What makes Canary Island vineyards unique compared to the rest of Europe?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Nunca fueron afectados por la filoxera — conservan cepas centenarias sobre pie franco (raíz original)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: They were never affected by phylloxera — they preserve century-old vines on original rootstock
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Una plaga del siglo XIX
distractors: Que se cultivan a mayor altitud que cualquier otro viñedo europeo, lo que obliga a vendimiar siempre de noche para preservar el frescor de la uva.
Que emplean solo variedades francesas replantadas tras la filoxera y luego adaptadas poco a poco al clima atlántico de las islas durante el último siglo.
distractors_en: That they grow at a higher altitude than any other European vineyard, which forces every single harvest to be carried out at night to keep the fruit fresh.
That they use only French varieties replanted after phylloxera and then slowly adapted to the islands' Atlantic climate over the course of the last century.

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿A qué temperatura se sirve un tinto con crianza?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: At what temperature is an oak-aged red served?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Ni frío ni caliente, por debajo de la temperatura ambiente
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: Neither cold nor warm, below room temperature

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿A qué temperatura se sirve un espumoso o champagne?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: At what temperature is sparkling wine or champagne served?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Bien frío

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué son los taninos?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What are tannins?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Compuestos de la piel, semillas y barrica que dan estructura y sensación de sequedad en boca
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Compounds from the skin, seeds and barrel that give structure and a drying sensation in the mouth
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Esa sensación áspera en la lengua
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: That rough feeling on your tongue
distractors: Los azúcares naturales que quedan en el vino cuando se detiene la fermentación, que aportan dulzor y una sensación más redonda y envolvente en el paladar.
Las burbujas de dióxido de carbono que libera una segunda fermentación en botella, que aportan frescura y una chispa viva al vino cuando se sirve en la copa.
distractors_en: The natural sugars left in the wine when fermentation stops, which bring sweetness and a rounder, fuller and more enveloping sensation on the palate.
The tiny bubbles of carbon dioxide released by a second fermentation in the bottle, which bring freshness and a lively prickle to the wine when it is served.

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Por qué se sujeta la copa por el tallo?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: Why do you hold a wine glass by the stem?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Para no calentar el vino con la mano y mantener la temperatura ideal
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: To avoid warming the wine with your hand and maintain the ideal temperature
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Tiene que ver con la temperatura

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Cuándo es necesario decantar un vino?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: When is it necessary to decant a wine?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Tintos jóvenes potentes (para suavizarlos) y vinos añejos (para separar sedimentos)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Powerful young reds (to soften them) and aged wines (to separate sediment)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Dos situaciones opuestas

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿De qué uva está hecho el Cepado?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What grape is Cepado made from?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Godello, de la DO Valdeorras (Galicia)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Godello, from DO Valdeorras (Galicia)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Una uva gallega blanca

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué hace especial al Castillo Ygay Gran Reserva Especial 2011?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What makes the Castillo Ygay Gran Reserva Especial 2011 special?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Es un Gran Reserva icónico de Marqués de Murrieta, Rioja. Tempranillo y Mazuelo. 590€
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: It's an iconic Gran Reserva by Marqués de Murrieta, Rioja. Tempranillo and Mazuelo. 590€
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: El tinto más caro de nuestra carta (sin contar magnums)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: The most expensive red on our list (excluding magnums)

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué vinos de Tempos Vega Sicilia tenemos?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: Which Tempos Vega Sicilia wines do we have?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Oremus Mandolás (blanco, Hungría), Alion (tinto, Ribera del Duero), Oremus Late Harvest y Aszú (dulces, Hungría)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Oremus Mandolás (white, Hungary), Alion (red, Ribera del Duero), Oremus Late Harvest and Aszú (dessert, Hungary)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Un grupo con bodegas en España y Hungría
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: A group with wineries in Spain and Hungary

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Cuál es nuestro vino kosher tinto? ¿Y el blanco?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What is our kosher red wine? And white?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Tinto: Les Lauriers (Burdeos) y Château By Medoc. No tenemos blanco kosher.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Red: Les Lauriers (Bordeaux) and Château By Medoc. We don't have a kosher white.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Son vinos franceses

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué vinos tenemos disponibles por copa? Nombra al menos 5.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: Which wines do we have available by the glass? Name at least 5.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Valtravieso Nogara, Fenomenal SB, El Grifo Lías, Muga Rosado, Arienzo, Cair Cuvèe, Artifice tinto, Marqués de Murrieta Reserva, Colet A Priori, Andre Clouet
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Valtravieso Nogara, Fenomenal SB, El Grifo Lías, Muga Rosado, Arienzo, Cair Cuvèe, Artifice tinto, Marqués de Murrieta Reserva, Colet A Priori, Andre Clouet
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Tenemos blancos, rosado, tintos y espumosos por copa
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: We have whites, rosé, reds and sparkling by the glass
distractors: Tempranillo, Garnacha, Albariño, Godello, Verdejo, Mencía, Monastrell, Bobal, Cariñena, Listán Blanco, Malvasía Volcánica, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot y Chardonnay.
DO Ca Rioja, Ribera del Duero, Rías Baixas, Rueda, Bierzo, Toro, Jumilla, Priorat, Penedès, Ycoden-Daute-Isora, Tacoronte-Acentejo, Valdeorras, La Orotava y Abona.
distractors_en: Tempranillo, Garnacha, Albariño, Godello, Verdejo, Mencía, Monastrell, Bobal, Cariñena, Listán Blanco, Malvasía Volcánica, Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot and Chardonnay.
DO Ca Rioja, Ribera del Duero, Rías Baixas, Rueda, Bierzo, Toro, Jumilla, Priorat, Penedès, Ycoden-Daute-Isora, Tacoronte-Acentejo, Valdeorras, La Orotava and Abona.

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿De qué uva es el Pazo Barrantes Gran Vino?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What grape is Pazo Barrantes Gran Vino made from?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Albariño, DO Rías Baixas (Galicia)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Albariño, DO Rías Baixas (Galicia)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: La uva blanca gallega más famosa

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Cuál es la diferencia entre Alion 2020 y Alion 2018 3L?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What is the difference between Alion 2020 and Alion 2018 3L?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: El 2020 es botella estándar (200€). El 2018 es Doble Magnum (3 litros) por 1210€. Ambos son Tinto Fino de Ribera del Duero.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: The 2020 is a standard bottle (200€). The 2018 is a Double Magnum (3 litres) at 1210€. Both are Tinto Fino from Ribera del Duero.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: El formato y la añada

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué vino recomendarías con el Tataki de atún?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What wine would you recommend with tuna tataki?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Blancos frescos y minerales: Cepado (Godello), Viñatigo Blanco, Fenomenal SB (Sauvignon Blanc) o un espumoso como Colet A Priori
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Fresh mineral whites: Cepado (Godello), Viñatigo Blanco, Fenomenal SB (Sauvignon Blanc) or a sparkling like Colet A Priori
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Acidez y frescura para el pescado
distractors: Tintos potentes y con crianza como Marqués de Murrieta Reserva, Contino Reserva o Alion, cuyo cuerpo tánico apaga el atún y el sésamo en lugar de realzarlos.
Un vino dulce de postre como Oremus Late Harvest o un Pedro Ximénez, para contrastar el sésamo tostado con notas intensas de miel, pasas y fruta confitada.
distractors_en: Powerful aged reds such as Marqués de Murrieta Reserva, Contino Reserva or Alion, whose tannic body overpowers the tuna and the sesame instead of lifting them.
A sweet dessert wine such as Oremus Late Harvest or a Pedro Ximénez, to contrast the toasted sesame with intense notes of honey, raisins and candied fruit.

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué vino va mejor con el Tartar de solomillo?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What wine pairs best with beef tenderloin tartare?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Tintos con cuerpo y crianza: Marqués de Murrieta Reserva, Contino Reserva, Alion 2020, Côte d'Or Bourgogne
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Full-bodied aged reds: Marqués de Murrieta Reserva, Contino Reserva, Alion 2020, Côte d'Or Bourgogne
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Tintos con estructura para carne roja

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué vino sugerirías con el Arroz negro con txipirones?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What wine would you suggest with black rice and squid?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Blancos con cuerpo: Remelluri Blanco, Amaren, Placet. O tintos elegantes: Viña Cubillo, Predicador
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Full-bodied whites: Remelluri Blanco, Amaren, Placet. Or elegant reds: Viña Cubillo, Predicador
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: El umami del mar pide vino con cuerpo
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: Ocean umami needs a wine with body

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: Un huésped pide Brownie de avellanas de postre. ¿Qué vino le ofreces?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: A guest orders hazelnut brownie for dessert. What wine do you offer?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Oremus Late Harvest o Tokaji Oremus Aszú 3 Puttonyos — vinos dulces húngaros que equilibran el chocolate
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Oremus Late Harvest or Tokaji Oremus Aszú 3 Puttonyos — Hungarian sweet wines that balance the chocolate
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Vinos dulces de Tokaji

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: ¿Qué vino iría bien con los Mejillones a la brasa?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: What wine would go well with grilled mussels?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Blancos aromáticos frescos: El Grifo Lías (Malvasía), Artifice Vidueño, o un rosado como Muga Rosado
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Fresh aromatic whites: El Grifo Lías (Malvasía), Artifice Vidueño, or a rosé like Muga Rosado
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Algo ligero y aromático para el marisco
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: Something light and aromatic for seafood

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: Un huésped pregunta: "¿Qué tiene de especial el vino canario?" — ¿Qué le dices?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: A guest asks: "What's special about Canary Island wine?" — What do you say?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Nuestros vinos canarios nacen de viñedos volcánicos centenarios que nunca fueron afectados por la filoxera. Son cepas con raíces originales, únicas en Europa. El suelo de lava y el viento del Atlántico les dan un carácter mineral y salino inigualable.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Our Canary wines come from century-old volcanic vineyards never affected by phylloxera. They are vines with original roots, unique in Europe. The lava soil and Atlantic wind give them an unmatched mineral, saline character.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Volcán + filoxera + Atlántico
distractors: Que son vinos jóvenes y económicos, pensados sobre todo para el turismo de playa, que se elaboran en grandes bodegas industriales modernas repartidas por todas las islas. Se hacen con variedades francesas e italianas traídas hace pocas décadas. Su estilo busca imitar a los grandes vinos europeos de siempre.

Que destacan por servirse siempre muy fríos y con mucho gas, porque el calor de las islas obliga a añadirles burbujas y azúcar para que resulten más refrescantes. Se elaboran en cooperativas jóvenes sin apenas historia. Por eso son vinos sencillos, pensados para beber deprisa junto al mar.
distractors_en: That they are young, inexpensive wines made mostly for beach tourism, produced in large modern industrial wineries spread across every one of the islands. They are made from French and Italian varieties brought over just a few decades ago. Their style simply tries to imitate the great classic European wines.

That they stand out for always being served very cold and fizzy, because the islands' heat forces producers to add bubbles and sugar to make them more refreshing. They come from young cooperatives with almost no history. They are simple wines, meant to be drunk quickly by the sea.

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: Un huésped quiere un tinto español bueno pero no quiere gastar más de 100€. ¿Qué le recomiendas?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: A guest wants a good Spanish red but doesn't want to spend over 100€. What do you recommend?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Varias opciones excelentes: Viña Cubillo 2016 (95€, Rioja), Amaren Selección (90€, Rioja), Paco Garcia La Villa (100€, Rioja), Montebaco (90€, Ribera del Duero) o Les Cousins L'Inconscient (80€, Priorat)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Several excellent options: Viña Cubillo 2016 (95€, Rioja), Amaren Selección (90€, Rioja), Paco Garcia La Villa (100€, Rioja), Montebaco (90€, Ribera del Duero) or Les Cousins L'Inconscient (80€, Priorat)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Piensa en Rioja, Ribera y Priorat

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: Una mesa quiere probar varios vinos sin pedir botellas completas. ¿Qué les ofreces?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: A table wants to try several wines without ordering full bottles. What do you offer?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Nuestra selección de vinos por copa: espumosos (Colet A Priori 15€, Andre Clouet 25€), blancos (Valtravieso 10€, Fenomenal SB 15€, El Grifo 20€), rosado (Muga 12€) y tintos (desde Arienzo 10€ hasta Murrieta 17€)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Our wines by the glass selection: sparkling (Colet A Priori 15€, Andre Clouet 25€), whites (Valtravieso 10€, Fenomenal SB 15€, El Grifo 20€), rosé (Muga 12€) and reds (from Arienzo 10€ to Murrieta 17€)
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Tenemos 10 vinos por copa de todos los tipos
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint_en: We have 10 wines by the glass of all types

WINE_FLASHCARDS.q: Un huésped dice que no le gusta el vino. ¿Qué alternativa le ofreces?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.q_en: A guest says they don't like wine. What alternative do you offer?
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a: Nuestra carta de cocktails (16.50€ cada uno) o mocktails sin alcohol (12€). Destacar el Aperol Spritz, Espresso Martini, o los mocktails Ipanema y Vitalía para opciones refrescantes.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.a_en: Our cocktail menu (16.50€ each) or non-alcoholic mocktails (12€). Highlight the Aperol Spritz, Espresso Martini, or the Ipanema and Vitalía mocktails for refreshing options.
WINE_FLASHCARDS.hint: Cocktails y mocktails
